title: calochortus umbellatus
intro: Calochortus umbellatus, commonly known as the golden star or star-tulip, is a perennial plant species native to the western United States and Canada. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant's roots and tubers are used to make herbal preparations, such as teas and tinctures, which are believed to offer health benefits such as reducing fever and alleviating digestive issues. In herbal medicine, Calochortus umbellatus is valued for its potential to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
